2026-04-09T05:28:37.0325991Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-09T05:26:47.840023057Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":57977,\"title\":\"[AgentServer] Docs: Document ItemExtensions.GetInputText and resolveReferences parameter in README\",\"body\":\"## Documentation Gap\\n\\n**Package:** `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses`\\n**Service directory:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/`\\n**Triggered by:** commit a9a95cda (PR #57974) by `@RaviPidaparthi`\\n\\n## What Changed\\n\\nPR #57974 refactored `ResponseContext.GetInputItemsAsync()` to return `IReadOnlyList` (was `IReadOnlyList`), added a `resolveReferences` parameter to both `GetInputItemsAsync` and `GetInputTextAsync`, and introduced the new public `ItemExtensions.GetInputText(IEnumerable)` extension class. The README was partially updated (convenience generators section and EchoHandler snippet were refreshed) but the following new public APIs are not yet documented.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- `ItemExtensions.GetInputText(this IEnumerable)` is a new public extension method (in `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses.Models`) that lets handlers extract text from any `IEnumerable` without going through `ResponseContext`. It is listed in the CHANGELOG but absent from the README Key concepts or Examples sections.\\n- The `resolveReferences` parameter on `GetInputItemsAsync(bool resolveReferences = true, ...)` and `GetInputTextAsync(bool resolveReferences = true, ...)` is undocumented in the README. Users need to know that passing `resolveReferences: false` returns `ItemReferenceParam` entries unresolved (useful for forwarding raw references without a round-trip to the provider).\\n- The README\\u0026#39;s Key concepts section describes `ResponseContext` only in a code comment (`// Returns IReadOnlyList`) but does not explain the semantic difference between `GetInputTextAsync` (text extraction shortcut) and `GetInputItemsAsync` (raw item access for multi-modal or structured input).\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n**File:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/README.md` — edit\\n\\nIn the **Key concepts** → **ResponseHandler** section, after the existing `ResponseEventStream` convenience generators table (the paragraph ending with links to Sample 3 and Sample 4), insert a new subsection:\\n\\n```markdown\\n### ResponseContext — input access\\n\\n`ResponseContext` exposes two complementary methods for reading the request\\u0026#39;s input items:\\n\\n| Method | Returns | Use when |\\n|--------|---------|----------|\\n| `GetInputTextAsync()` | `Task` | You only need the plain text from the input (most common) |\\n| `GetInputItemsAsync()` | `Task\\u0026gt;` | You need structured items (images, function results, references) |\\n\\nBoth methods accept an optional `resolveReferences` parameter (default `true`). Pass `resolveReferences: false` to receive `ItemReferenceParam` entries without resolving them — useful when forwarding raw references to an upstream model without a provider round-trip.\\n\\nThe `GetInputText(this IEnumerable)` extension on `ItemExtensions` can extract text from any sequence of `Item` objects you have already fetched:\\n\\n```\\n// Assuming you retrieved items separately (e.g., combined history + input):\\nvar inputItems = await context.GetInputItemsAsync(cancellationToken=[REDACTED] historyItems = await context.GetHistoryAsync(cancellationToken=[REDACTED]    .ContinueWith(t =\\u0026gt; t.Result.Select(o =\\u0026gt; o as Item).Where(i =\\u0026gt; i != null).Cast());\\n\\nvar allText = inputItems.Concat(historyItems).GetInputText();\\n```\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\n1. **Write the sample code** in `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/tests/Snippets/ReadMeSnippets.cs`\\n\\n   Add a new test method:\\n\\n   ```csharp\\n   #region Snippet:Responses_ReadMe_ItemExtensions_GetInputText\\n   [Test]\\n   public async Task GetInputText_FromItemSequence()\\n   {\\n       // Combine input items and resolved history for text extraction.\\n       var context = new MockResponseContext(\\u0026#34;resp-123\\u0026#34;);\\n       var inputItems = await context.GetInputItemsAsync();\\n       var text = inputItems.GetInputText();\\n       Console.WriteLine(text);\\n   }\\n   #endregion\\n   ```\\n\\n2. **README placeholder** is already shown above in Step 1 with the correct `` ```C# Snippet:Responses_ReadMe_ItemExtensions_GetInputText `` fence. Run `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 agentserver` after adding the test method.\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe README already contains all six required sections. No structural changes needed — only the content additions described in Step 1.\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order:\\n\\n1. `dotnet build sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/`\\n2. `dotnet test sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\\n3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 agentserver`\\n4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 agentserver` — only needed if the snippet test method adds new public types (it should not)\\n5. `dotnet format sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/`\\n\\n\\n\\n## Next Steps\\n\\n\\u0026gt; [!TIP]\\n\\u0026gt; **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above\\n\\n\\n\\n\\n\\u0026gt; Generated by [Update Docs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/actions/runs/24173776728/agentic_workflow) · ● 319.3K · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3AAzure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net+is%3Aissue+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+Azure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net%2Fupdate-samples-and-docs%22\\u0026amp;type=issues)\\n\\n\\n\\n\\n\",\"state\":\"open\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57977\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"github-actions[bot]\",\"id\":41898282,\"profile_url\":\"https://github.com/apps/github-actions\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/15368?v=4\"},\"author_association\":\"NONE\",\"reactions\":{\"total_count\":0,\"+1\":0,\"-1\":0,\"laugh\":0,\"confused\":0,\"heart\":0,\"hooray\":0,\"rocket\":0,\"eyes\":0},\"created_at\":\"2026-04-09T05:23:51Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-04-09T05:23:51Z\"}","type":"text"}]}}}
2026-04-09T05:28:37.0326954Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-09T05:26:47.85324647Z","direction":"OUT","type":"REQUEST","server_id":"github","method":"tools/call","payload":{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"arguments":{"issue_number":57977,"method":"get","owner":"Azure","repo":"azure-sdk-for-net"},"name":"issue_read"}}}
2026-04-09T05:28:37.0350397Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-09T05:26:48.004209963Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":57977,\"title\":\"[AgentServer] Docs: Document ItemExtensions.GetInputText and resolveReferences parameter in README\",\"body\":\"## Documentation Gap\\n\\n**Package:** `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses`\\n**Service directory:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/`\\n**Triggered by:** commit a9a95cda (PR #57974) by `@RaviPidaparthi`\\n\\n## What Changed\\n\\nPR #57974 refactored `ResponseContext.GetInputItemsAsync()` to return `IReadOnlyList` (was `IReadOnlyList`), added a `resolveReferences` parameter to both `GetInputItemsAsync` and `GetInputTextAsync`, and introduced the new public `ItemExtensions.GetInputText(IEnumerable)` extension class. The README was partially updated (convenience generators section and EchoHandler snippet were refreshed) but the following new public APIs are not yet documented.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- `ItemExtensions.GetInputText(this IEnumerable)` is a new public extension method (in `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses.Models`) that lets handlers extract text from any `IEnumerable` without going through `ResponseContext`. It is listed in the CHANGELOG but absent from the README Key concepts or Examples sections.\\n- The `resolveReferences` parameter on `GetInputItemsAsync(bool resolveReferences = true, ...)` and `GetInputTextAsync(bool resolveReferences = true, ...)` is undocumented in the README. Users need to know that passing `resolveReferences: false` returns `ItemReferenceParam` entries unresolved (useful for forwarding raw references without a round-trip to the provider).\\n- The README\\u0026#39;s Key concepts section describes `ResponseContext` only in a code comment (`// Returns IReadOnlyList`) but does not explain the semantic difference between `GetInputTextAsync` (text extraction shortcut) and `GetInputItemsAsync` (raw item access for multi-modal or structured input).\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n**File:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/README.md` — edit\\n\\nIn the **Key concepts** → **ResponseHandler** section, after the existing `ResponseEventStream` convenience generators table (the paragraph ending with links to Sample 3 and Sample 4), insert a new subsection:\\n\\n```markdown\\n### ResponseContext — input access\\n\\n`ResponseContext` exposes two complementary methods for reading the request\\u0026#39;s input items:\\n\\n| Method | Returns | Use when |\\n|--------|---------|----------|\\n| `GetInputTextAsync()` | `Task` | You only need the plain text from the input (most common) |\\n| `GetInputItemsAsync()` | `Task\\u0026gt;` | You need structured items (images, function results, references) |\\n\\nBoth methods accept an optional `resolveReferences` parameter (default `true`). Pass `resolveReferences: false` to receive `ItemReferenceParam` entries without resolving them — useful when forwarding raw references to an upstream model without a provider round-trip.\\n\\nThe `GetInputText(this IEnumerable)` extension on `ItemExtensions` can extract text from any sequence of `Item` objects you have already fetched:\\n\\n```\\n// Assuming you retrieved items separately (e.g., combined history + input):\\nvar inputItems = await context.GetInputItemsAsync(cancellationToken=[REDACTED] historyItems = await context.GetHistoryAsync(cancellationToken=[REDACTED]    .ContinueWith(t =\\u0026gt; t.Result.Select(o =\\u0026gt; o as Item).Where(i =\\u0026gt; i != null).Cast());\\n\\nvar allText = inputItems.Concat(historyItems).GetInputText();\\n```\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\n1. **Write the sample code** in `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/tests/Snippets/ReadMeSnippets.cs`\\n\\n   Add a new test method:\\n\\n   ```csharp\\n   #region Snippet:Responses_ReadMe_ItemExtensions_GetInputText\\n   [Test]\\n   public async Task GetInputText_FromItemSequence()\\n   {\\n       // Combine input items and resolved history for text extraction.\\n       var context = new MockResponseContext(\\u0026#34;resp-123\\u0026#34;);\\n       var inputItems = await context.GetInputItemsAsync();\\n       var text = inputItems.GetInputText();\\n       Console.WriteLine(text);\\n   }\\n   #endregion\\n   ```\\n\\n2. **README placeholder** is already shown above in Step 1 with the correct `` ```C# Snippet:Responses_ReadMe_ItemExtensions_GetInputText `` fence. Run `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 agentserver` after adding the test method.\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe README already contains all six required sections. No structural changes needed — only the content additions described in Step 1.\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order:\\n\\n1. `dotnet build sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/`\\n2. `dotnet test sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\\n3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 agentserver`\\n4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 agentserver` — only needed if the snippet test method adds new public types (it should not)\\n5. `dotnet format sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/`\\n\\n\\n\\n## Next Steps\\n\\n\\u0026gt; [!TIP]\\n\\u0026gt; **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above\\n\\n\\n\\n\\n\\u0026gt; Generated by [Update Docs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/actions/runs/24173776728/agentic_workflow) · ● 319.3K · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3AAzure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net+is%3Aissue+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+Azure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net%2Fupdate-samples-and-docs%22\\u0026amp;type=issues)\\n\\n\\n\\n\\n\",\"state\":\"open\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57977\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"github-actions[bot]\",\"id\":41898282,\"profile_url\":\"https://github.com/apps/github-actions\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/15368?v=4\"},\"author_association\":\"NONE\",\"reactions\":{\"total_count\":0,\"+1\":0,\"-1\":0,\"laugh\":0,\"confused\":0,\"heart\":0,\"hooray\":0,\"rocket\":0,\"eyes\":0},\"created_at\":\"2026-04-09T05:23:51Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-04-09T05:23:51Z\"}","type":"text"}]}}}

2026-04-09T05:28:37.0426335Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-09T05:27:30.277127163Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"total_count\":23,\"incomplete_results\":false,\"items\":[{\"id\":4090517017,\"number\":57183,\"state\":\"closed\",\"state_reason\":\"completed\",\"locked\":false,\"title\":\"[BUG] Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent NullReferenceException after successful streamed response finalization\",\"body\":\"## Bug description\\nWhen using `Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent` through Microsoft Agent Framework streaming APIs, a chat run can complete successfully, return the full assistant answer, and then crash during stream finalization/disposal with a `System.NullReferenceException`.\\n\\nThe exception originates inside the Azure SDK telemetry/finalization path:\\n`Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.RunStepOpenAPIToolCall.JsonModelWriteCore(...)`\\n\\n## Package and environment\\n- Package: `Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent`\\n- Package version: `1.0.0-preview.260311.1`\\n- App shape: .NET 10 console app using Microsoft Agent Framework with Azure Foundry persistent agents\\n- OS: Windows\\n- Auth=[REDACTED] Streaming path: `AIAgent.RunStreamingAsync(...)`\\n\\n## Expected behavior\\nAfter the streamed answer completes, enumeration/disposal should finish cleanly.\\n\\n## Actual behavior\\nThe full assistant answer is returned to the caller, but the process crashes afterward during stream cleanup/final telemetry processing.\\n\\n## Reproduction notes\\n1. Create a persistent Foundry agent and connect with `PersistentAgentsClient` / `ChatClientAgent`.\\n2. Wrap the agent with Agent Framework OpenTelemetry instrumentation.\\n3. Call `RunStreamingAsync(...)` and enumerate the response stream to completion.\\n4. Observe that, after the final assistant output is emitted, the SDK throws `NullReferenceException` during finalization.\\n\\n## Minimal observed output\\n```text\\nassistant(stream)\\u003e \\u003cfull answer omitted\\u003e\\n[update] role=assistant, messageId=..., author=..., responseId=...\\n[update] role=assistant, messageId=..., author=..., responseId=...\\n[content:UsageContent] Microsoft.Extensions.AI.UsageContent\\nUnhandled exception. System.NullReferenceException: Object reference not set to an instance of an object.\\n   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.RunStepOpenAPIToolCall.JsonModelWriteCore(Utf8JsonWriter writer, ModelReaderWriterOptions options)\\n   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.RunStepOpenAPIToolCall.System.ClientModel.Primitives.IJsonModel\\u003cAzure.AI.Agents.Persistent.RunStepOpenAPIToolCall\\u003e.Write(...)\\n   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.ModelSerializationExtensions.WriteObjectValue[T](...)\\n   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.RunStepOpenAPIToolCall.ToRequestContent()\\n   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.Telemetry.OpenTelemetryScope.GetToolCallAttributes(RunStepToolCall toolCall)\\n   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.Telemetry.OpenTelemetryScope.ProcessToolCalls(RunStepToolCallDetails toolCallDetails)\\n   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.Telemetry.OpenTelemetryScope.RecordRunStepEventAttributes(RunStep runStep, Boolean stream)\\n   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.Telemetry.OpenTelemetryScope.End()\\n   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.Telemetry.OpenTelemetryScope.Dispose()\\n```\\n\\n## Suspected root cause\\nThe generated serializer for `RunStepOpenAPIToolCall` appears to iterate `OpenAPI` without a null check.\\n\\nRelevant file: `sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent/src/Generated/RunStepOpenAPIToolCall.Serialization.cs`\\n\\n## Impact\\n- The user-visible answer is already complete, but the client process still terminates.\\n- This makes streamed chat runs unreliable in production.\\n- The issue appears tied to SDK finalization/telemetry, not application rendering.\\n\\n## Workaround currently used\\nApplication-side workaround: catch this very specific `NullReferenceException` after the stream completes and preserve the completed answer.\\n\\n## Request\\nPlease confirm whether this is a known bug in `Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent` preview and whether a null-safe fix should be added around `RunStepOpenAPIToolCall` serialization / telemetry attribute
